zh.lifeSummary: 當William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby於大約1242年出生在英國越可爾馬爾頓時，他的父親William Latimer24歲，母親Alice Hansard37歲。他於1268年與Alice Ledet在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1270年登記服兵役。他於1300年被列為keeper of berwick castle。他去世於1304年12月5日在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾可比享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾爾普林加姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby于大约1242年出生在英国越可爾馬爾頓时，他的父亲William Latimer24岁，母亲Alice Hansard37岁。他于1268年与Alice Ledet在英国苏格兰阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1270年登记服兵役。他于1300年被列为keeper of berwick castle。他去世于1304年12月5日在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾可比享年63岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾爾普林加姆。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Latimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a Latinist, a clerk who wrote documents in Latin, from Anglo-Norman French latinier, latim(m)ier. Latin was more or less the universal language of official documents in the Middle Ages, displaced only gradually by the vernacular – in England, by Anglo-Norman French at first, and eventually by English.

variants.name: Latner
meanings: English: occupational name for a worker in or maker of latten or brass, from Middle English latoun ‘brass’ (Old French laton). This name may sometimes have been confused with Latimer .
Altered form of German   Lattner   or   Lettner  .

variants.name: Lattimore
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Latimore
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Lattimer
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Lattner
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .
German: occupational name for a carpenter who prepared or used laths or slats, from Middle High German latte ‘slat, lath’ + -n (plural suffix) + the agent suffix -er.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby was born about 1242, in Malton, Yorkshire, England, his father, William Latimer, was 24 and his mother, Alice Hansard, was 37. He married Alice Ledet in 1268, in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1270. In 1300, at the age of 59, his occupation is listed as keeper of berwick castle. He died on 5 December 1304, in Corby, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Helpringham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby est né environ 1242, à Malton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William Latimer, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Alice Hansard, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Alice Ledet en 1268, à Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1270. En 1300, à l’âge de 59 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est keeper of berwick castle. Il est décédé le 5 décembre 1304, à Corby, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Helpringham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby um 1242 in Malton, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Latimer 24 und seine Mutter Alice Hansard 37. Er hat 1268 in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Alice Ledet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1270 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1300, als er 59 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als keeper of berwick castle aufgeführt. Er starb am 5. Dezember 1304 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Corby, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Helpringham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby è nato il circa 1242 nel Malton, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, William Latimer, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Alice Hansard, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Alice Ledet nel 1268, nel Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1270. Nel 1300, all'età di 59 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come keeper of berwick castle. Morì il 5 dicembre 1304, nel Corby, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Helpringham, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corbyが約1242年にイギリスヨークシュアイアマルトンで生まれた時，父William Latimerは24歳，母Alice Hansardは37歳であった。1268年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアブチャンでAlice Ledetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1270年に兵役登録をした。1300年，59歳の時，職業はkeeper of berwick castleと記録されている。彼は1304年12月5日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアコルビーで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアヘルプリンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby(이)가 대략 1242년 영국 요르크실르 맬턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Latimer(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Hansard(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1268년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 버챈에서 Alice Ledet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1270년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1300년, 59세의 나이로 그의 직업은 keeper of berwick castle이었다. 그는 1304년 12월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 코르비에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 헬프링함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby nasceu aproximadamente 1242, em Malton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Latimer, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Alice Hansard, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Ledet em 1268, em Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1270. Em 1300, com 59 anos, sua profissão aparece como keeper of berwick castle. Ele faleceu em 5 de dezembro de 1304, em Corby, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Helpringham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby родился около 1242 г. в местности малтон, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Latimer было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Hansard, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Ledet в 1268 г. в местности бучань, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. В 1270 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1300 году, в возрасте 59 лет, работал по профессии keeper of berwick castle. Он умер 5 декабря 1304 г. в местности корби, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности хелпрингхам, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby nació aproximadamente 1242, en Malton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Latimer, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Alice Hansard, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Alice Ledet en 1268, en Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1270. En 1300, a la edad de 59 años, su ocupación registrada era keeper of berwick castle. Murió el 5 de diciembre de 1304, en Corby, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Helpringham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
